title: The Seasons' Comfort
content:
O Summer sun, O moving trees!
    O cheerful human noise, O busy glittering street!
    What hour shall Fate in all the future find,
    Or what delights, ever to equal these:
    Only to taste the warmth, the light, the wind,
    Only to be alive, and feel that life is sweet?
meaning:
Robert Laurence Binyon's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Seasons' Comfort"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
